title: Adapt build_viewer.py for NeurIPS data format
description:
MAJOR TASK: Adapt build_viewer.py (1,461 lines) to work with NeurIPS data.

DATA FORMAT MAPPING (ASH → NeurIPS):
- paper_id → id
- title → name
- authors (string) → authors (array of {id, fullname, institution})
- abstract_text → abstract
- disease_topics (array) → topic (single string like 'Computer Vision->Everything Else')
- session_title → session
- session_type → eventtype
- session_room → room_name
- session_datetime → starttime
- url → paper_url

NEW FIELDS in NeurIPS to use:
- decision: 'Accept (Oral)', 'Accept (poster)', 'Accept (spotlight)' → filter + badge
- poster_position: '#4902' → display in card
- endtime: session end time → show duration
- sourceurl: OpenReview track URL
- virtualsite_url: NeurIPS virtual site link

FILTER CHANGES NEEDED:
- Remove 'program' filter (ASH-specific)
- Add 'decision' filter (Oral/Poster/Spotlight)
- Change 'topics' from array to single topic
- Keep day filtering (extract from starttime)

LOAD FUNCTION CHANGES:
- Current load_abstracts() reads from abstracts_YEAR/ directory
- Need new load_neurips() that reads from neurips-2025-orals-posters.json
- Handle authors array → display string conversion

TEXT REPLACEMENTS:
- 'ASH' → 'NeurIPS' throughout template
- 'View on ASH ↗' → 'View on OpenReview ↗'
- localStorage key 'ash__YEAR___starred' → 'neurips__YEAR__'

COMMIT after completing this task.

title: Adapt enrich_embeddings.py for NeurIPS data
description:
Adapt the embedding enrichment script to work with NeurIPS data format:

1. Update to read from neurips-2025-orals-posters.json instead of abstracts_YEAR/ directory
2. Map NeurIPS fields (name, abstract, topic, authors array) to embedding input
3. Generate embeddings for all 6,002 papers
4. Compute similarity matrix
5. Output similarity_2025.json in same format as before

The script uses OpenAI embeddings API (check .env for API key).
This enables the 'Find Similar' feature in the viewer.

title: Initial git commit with clean NeurIPS repo
description:
After all cleanup is done, create initial commit:

1. Ensure .gitignore is appropriate
2. Stage all cleaned files
3. Commit with message like 'Initial NeurIPS 2025 Abstract Browser (converted from ASH)'
4. Do NOT push to old remote - user will set up new remote

This should be done after:
- ASH subdirectory removed
- ASH data files removed  
- Scripts cleaned up
- README/AGENTS updated

title: Generate and test NeurIPS viewer
description:
After adapting build_viewer.py:

1. Run: uv run scripts/build_viewer.py
2. Verify output: data/viewer_neurips2025.html (or similar naming)
3. Test in browser:
   - All 6,002 papers load correctly
   - Search works (title, abstract, authors)
   - Filters work (topic, decision, day)
   - Paper cards display correctly (title, authors, abstract)
   - OpenReview links work
   - Keyboard navigation (j/k, /, s, Enter)
   - Starring works and persists
   - Mobile view works

4. Check edge cases:
   - Papers with missing fields
   - Long abstracts
   - Special characters in titles/abstracts

title: Update AGENTS.md for NeurIPS
description:
Update AGENTS.md to reference NeurIPS instead of ASH:

Current line 1: '# Agent Instructions for ASH Abstract Browser'

Should become: '# Agent Instructions for NeurIPS Abstract Browser'

This is a minor text change but important for consistency.

title: Update README.md for NeurIPS
description:
Complete rewrite of README.md:

Current content references:
- 'ASH Abstract Browser'
- 'American Society of Hematology'
- submit.hematology.org and ash.confex.com URLs
- ASH-specific scraper scripts

New content should describe:
- NeurIPS 2025 Abstract Browser
- Link to deployed viewer (TBD)
- How to build the viewer
- Data source (neurips-2025-orals-posters.json)
- Viewer features (search, filter by topic, star papers)

title: Remove ash2025 subdirectory (archived ASH copy)
description: The ash2025/ subdirectory contains a full copy of the original ASH repository including its own .git, .beads, data, and scripts. This should be removed since we're starting fresh for NeurIPS. Contents: scripts for scraping ASH, 7,255 ASH abstracts, generated viewers.

title: Apply Apart Research-inspired design using frontend-design skill
description:
Use the frontend-design skill to create a distinctive, polished design for the NeurIPS viewer.

Design inspiration from Apart Research homepage:
- Bright neon green primary accent (#00FF7F or similar)
- Secondary colors: yellow, blue, coral/red
- Clean, modern sans-serif typography
- White/light background
- Simple, bold visual hierarchy
- Professional but vibrant feel

Apply to:
- Color scheme (CSS custom properties)
- Typography choices
- Card styling
- Button and filter styling
- Header/branding area
- Overall visual polish

This replaces the burgundy ASH medical theme with a fresh ML/AI research aesthetic.

title: Remove ASH-specific data files
description:
Remove ASH data that doesn't apply to NeurIPS:
- data/abstracts_2025/ (963 ASH abstract JSON files)
- data/paper_ids_2024.json (ASH 2024 metadata)
- data/similarity_2025.json (ASH similarity data)
- data/viewer_2024.html (ASH 2024 generated viewer)
- data/viewer_2025.html (ASH 2025 generated viewer)

Keep only: data/neurips-2025-orals-posters.json (6,002 NeurIPS papers, 26MB)

title: Generate NeurIPS embeddings and similarity data
description:
After adapting enrich_embeddings.py:

1. Run: uv run scripts/enrich_embeddings.py
2. Verify output: data/similarity_2025.json
3. Check similarity quality by spot-checking a few papers
4. Ensure the viewer correctly loads and uses similarity data

This may take a while due to API calls for 6,002 papers.

title: Update HTML template styling for NeurIPS
description:
Review and potentially update the HTML template styling in build_viewer.py: CURRENT STYLING (lines 86-100): - Accent color: #8B2942 (burgundy/maroon - ASH medical theme) - Font: Fraunces + Source Serif 4 (elegant serif fonts) CONSIDER FOR NEURIPS: - Accent color could change to more tech/ML theme (blue? purple?) - Fonts are nice and can stay - Dark mode support already exists This is optional polish - current styling will work fine. REQUIRED TEXT CHANGES: - Title: 'ASH __YEAR__ Abstracts' → 'NeurIPS __YEAR__' - Header: '

ASH __YEAR__

' → 'NeurIPS' - Help text and branding throughout

title: [EPIC] Convert ASH 2025 Abstract Browser to NeurIPS 2025
Remove or archive ASH scraper scripts
description:
Decide what to do with ASH-specific scripts:

scripts/scraper_2025.py (1,040 lines):
- Scrapes from submit.hematology.org
- Uses virtual-meeting.net API
- Not needed for NeurIPS (data already provided)

scripts/scraper_2024.py (389 lines):
- Scrapes from ash.confex.com
- Historical ASH 2024 data
- Not needed for NeurIPS

scripts/config.py (35 lines):
- Contains ASH event IDs and API endpoints
- Not needed for NeurIPS

Options:
1. Delete completely (cleanest)
2. Move to archive/ directory (preserves for reference)

Recommendation: Delete since ash2025/ subdirectory already has the archived version.

title: Final audit: Search for remaining ASH references
description:
FINAL CLEANUP TASK - Do this last before final commit.

Comprehensive search for any remaining ASH/hematology references:
- grep -ri 'ash' across all files
- grep -ri 'hematology' across all files  
- grep -ri 'blood' in code files (context-dependent)
- Check HTML output for any ASH branding
- Check localStorage keys
- Check URLs pointing to hematology sites

This ensures complete conversion with no leftover references.

title: Update .github workflows if needed
description:
Check .github/ directory for any ASH-specific workflows or references:
- GitHub Actions workflows
- Issue templates
- PR templates

These may reference ASH or old deployment targets and need updating.
